evidence_quotes.quote: Certain payment processors choose to annoy entire companies in order to remove perfectly legal contents hosted by them, applying censorship to various products including art and videogames.
Everything is a subscription, a rent, a license and not a permanent product in the hands of the consumer.
Another issue its the unilateral change of TOS, which can only be accepted in order to access any previously purchased product.
a videogame about violence isn't always about celebrating violence; most of the times it's a warning

paragraphs.text: I wish to point out how payment processors seem to have applied various forms of censorship to various platform such as: Steam, Getchu DL, Denpasoft, Pixiv, Toranoana, DLsite, Fantia, OnlyFans, Fanza, pictSPACE, Melonbooks, Aellune, Niconico, WikiLeaks, Pornhub, Itch, Patreon and more This is not fair. What's the point of owning money, if you can't use them because credit card companies censor content that they don't like, despite they are legal? If something is legal, I want to be allowed to buy it, regardless of what others thinks. I should be free to use my money how I wish, as long as I'm buying something legal. Certain payment processors choose to annoy entire companies in order to remove perfectly legal contents hosted by them, applying censorship to various products including art and videogames. Another problem that I want to remind you is the fact that nowadays we own almost nothing. Everything is a subscription, a rent, a license and not a permanent product in the hands of the consumer. Washing machines require a internet connection to work, most of them no longer have buttons, nor a display, they barely have only the button to turn them on and off. You need an app to use them, you need to give them access to your wifi and frequently you need to set the washing program from a cloud; that's extremely unsafe privacy-wise, it's not pratical because not having internet means that you can't wash and it's not consumer-friendly. It's the same for dishwashers, fridges and many other products. This also open a lot of new problems, such as what will happen if the company that make them and their app close or choose to no longer support them. This already happened with music, videogames, anime and more things... Another issue its the unilateral change of TOS, which can only be accepted in order to access any previously purchased product. There is no real choice, it's either accept or give up on something that you purchased. Going back to the online censorship topic, censorship directly undermines the right to free speech and expression. In democratic societies, free expression is considered a fundamental right because it allows individuals to share ideas, critique governments or institutions, and engage in open debate. When censorship limits these freedoms, it stifles creativity, progress, and the ability to challenge existing systems. It's a objective problem, especially when it start to affect economy by censoring certain companies because of "moral values", despite they are perfectly legal. Censoring someone because he make or like horror is like censoring someone because he is black or he like to draw black characters. The same is true in many fields. Censorship can limit the public's access to important or diverse information. When governments or organizations suppress certain content, they are controlling what people can learn, think, and believe. This can result in a narrow view of the world and prevent people from making informed decisions about their lives, societies, or the world and this is not how democracy works. People have to follow laws, not the moral values of private companies that choose what you can buy, what you can watch, read, listen and think. They cannot censor art, nor rewrite history for example not allowing purchasing certain books. Censorship is often used to silence marginalized groups or individuals whose views may challenge the status quo or dominant ideologies, and among the "censored products" there were many products about sensitive topics, including content related to LGBTQ+ community. When voices from minorities or dissenting groups are censored, it perpetuates inequality and prevents societal progress by not allowing those voices to be heard. Also, a videogame about violence isn't always about celebrating violence; most of the times it's a warning, a touching story about a victim, or about someone that can't accept to be a victim and try to fight back gaining freedom... Thanks for reading.
